maintained_note: Interpretation/trigger logic is hand-maintained by the project. Source URLs are auto-verified by verify_sources.py (see data/source_status.json). This file is the single editable source of truth for obligations; no code change is needed to update a rule.

obligations.effective_date_note: Art. 5 prohibitions apply from 2 Feb 2025 (6 months after the 1 Aug 2024 entry into force).
requirements: Emotion recognition in the workplace or education settings is prohibited (Art. 5(1)(f)).
Untargeted scraping of facial images to build recognition databases is prohibited (Art. 5(1)(e)).
Social scoring of natural persons by public or private actors is prohibited (Art. 5(1)(c)).
If any prohibited practice is present, the system cannot be placed on the EU market.
obligations.penalty: Up to EUR 35,000,000 or 7% of total worldwide annual turnover, whichever is higher (Art. 99).

obligations.regime: EU AI Act -- High-risk system (Article 6, Annex III)
obligations.effective_date_note: Annex III high-risk obligations apply from 2 Aug 2026 (24 months after entry into force); Annex I from 2 Aug 2027.
requirements: Establish and maintain a risk-management system (Art. 9).
Data governance: relevant, representative, error-checked training/validation data (Art. 10).
Technical documentation and automatic logging/record-keeping (Arts. 11-12).
Transparency and instructions for use to deployers (Art. 13).
Effective human oversight by design (Art. 14).
Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity (Art. 15).
Conformity assessment, CE marking, and registration in the EU database before market placement.
obligations.penalty: Up to EUR 15,000,000 or 3% of total worldwide annual turnover (Art. 99).

obligations.effective_date_note: Art. 50 transparency duties apply from 2 Aug 2026 (24 months after entry into force).
requirements: Inform natural persons that they are interacting with an AI system, unless obvious (Art. 50(1)).
Mark AI-generated or manipulated audio, image, video or text as artificially generated, in a machine-readable way (Art. 50(2), 50(4)).
Disclose deep-fake content as artificially generated or manipulated.
obligations.penalty: Up to EUR 15,000,000 or 3% of worldwide annual turnover (Art. 99).

obligations.regime: EU AI Act -- General-purpose AI models (Chapter V)
obligations.effective_date_note: GPAI obligations (Arts. 51-55) apply from 2 Aug 2025 (12 months after entry into force).
requirements: Maintain technical documentation and a summary of training content (Art. 53).
Put in place a policy to comply with EU copyright law (Art. 53).
Models with systemic risk (training compute > 10^25 FLOP) face additional evaluation, adversarial testing, incident reporting and cybersecurity duties (Art. 55).
obligations.penalty: Up to EUR 15,000,000 or 3% of worldwide annual turnover (Art. 101 for GPAI providers).

obligations.regime: NYC Local Law 144 -- Automated Employment Decision Tools
obligations.citation: NYC Local Law 144 of 2021; 6 RCNY Sec. 5-300 et seq. (DCWP Final Rule)
obligations.effective_date_note: DCWP began enforcement of Local Law 144 on 5 Jul 2023.
requirements: Commission an INDEPENDENT bias audit by an independent auditor no more than one year before using the tool (Local Law 144 Sec. 20-871(a)(1)).

The audit must compute the selection or scoring rate and the impact ratio across sex categories, race/ethnicity categories, AND intersectional sex x race/ethnicity categories; a category under 2% of the data may be excluded (DCWP Final Rule).
Use the AEDT's own historical use data; imputed or inferred demographic data may NOT be used (test data only where historical data is insufficient).

Publicly post, before use, a summary of the most recent bias audit including the audit date, the source/explanation of the data, the count of individuals in the unknown category, and the rates and impact ratios for all categories, plus the tool's distribution date (Sec. 20-871(a)(2)).

Notify each NYC-resident candidate/employee at least 10 business days before use, allow a request for an alternative selection process or accommodation, disclose the job qualifications the tool assesses, and on request disclose the data type/source/retention policy (Sec. 20-871(b)).
obligations.penalty: Civil penalty up to USD 500 for a first violation (and each additional violation the same day), and USD 500-1,500 for each subsequent violation. Each day of AEDT use, and each failure to give a required notice, is a separate violation (Local Law 144 Sec. 20-872).

obligations.regime: Colorado AI Act (SB 24-205) -- High-risk AI systems
obligations.citation: Colorado AI Act, Colo. Rev. Stat. Sec. 6-1-1701 et seq. (SB 24-205, 2024). EFFECTIVE-DATE MOVING TARGET: compliance start postponed from 1 Feb 2026 to 30 Jun 2026 by SB 25B-004 (Aug 2025); framework substantially revised by SB 26-189 (signed 14 May 2026, effective 1 Jan 2027). Confirm which regime governs your compliance date.
obligations.effective_date_note: MOVING TARGET: compliance start postponed from 1 Feb 2026 to 30 Jun 2026 by SB 25B-004 (Aug 2025); SB 26-189 (eff. 1 Jan 2027) substantially revises the framework.
requirements: Developers and deployers must use reasonable care to avoid algorithmic discrimination in high-risk systems making (or being a substantial factor in) consequential decisions.
Deployers must implement a risk-management policy and program, complete an impact assessment, and conduct an annual review for algorithmic discrimination.

Notify consumers when a high-risk system makes, or is a substantial factor in, a consequential decision, with an explanation; allow correction of personal data and appeal via human review where technically feasible.
Disclose to consumers when they are interacting with an AI system, unless it would be obvious to a reasonable person.
Report discovered algorithmic discrimination to the Colorado Attorney General within 90 days of discovery.
obligations.penalty: Enforced EXCLUSIVELY by the Colorado Attorney General as a deceptive trade practice under the Colorado Consumer Protection Act; no private right of action.

obligations.effective_date_note: NIST AI RMF 1.0 (AI 100-1) released 26 Jan 2023; voluntary framework, no statutory effective date.
requirements: Govern: establish a culture of AI risk management and accountability.
Map: establish context and identify risks of the AI system.
Measure: assess, analyse and track identified risks.
Manage: prioritise and act on risks; document residual risk.
Not legally binding, but widely used as the de-facto US benchmark and referenced by procurement.
obligations.penalty: None (voluntary). Often expected contractually or by enterprise customers.

obligations.regime: Interim Measures for Generative AI Services
obligations.citation: Sheng cheng shi ren gong zhi neng fu wu guan li zan xing ban fa (CAC et al.), effective 2023-08-15
obligations.effective_date_note: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative AI Services effective 15 Aug 2023.
requirements: Services with public-opinion or social-mobilisation attributes must complete a security assessment and file the algorithm with the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC).
Ensure legality of training data and respect for intellectual property and personal information.
Label AI-generated content and implement content moderation aligned with applicable rules.
obligations.penalty: Rectification orders, suspension of service, and penalties under the CSL/DSL/PIPL framework.

obligations.citation: Hu lian wang xin xi fu wu suan fa tui jian guan li gui ding (CAC et al.), effective 2022-03-01
obligations.effective_date_note: Provisions on the Management of Algorithmic Recommendations effective 1 Mar 2022.
requirements: File recommendation algorithms with public-opinion properties in the CAC algorithm registry.
Provide users an option to turn off algorithmic recommendation and to manage interest tags.
No unreasonable differential pricing ('big-data price discrimination') based on user profiles.
obligations.penalty: Warnings, rectification, fines and possible suspension under applicable cyber regulations.

obligations.regime: Provisions on Deep Synthesis / AI-content labelling
obligations.citation: Hu lian wang xin xi fu wu shen du he cheng guan li gui ding (2023-01-10); AI content-labelling measures (2025-09-01)
obligations.effective_date_note: Provisions on the Administration of Deep Synthesis effective 10 Jan 2023.
requirements: Conspicuously and machine-readably label deep-synthesis / AI-generated images, audio and video.
Filing and security-assessment duties for deep-synthesis service providers.
obligations.penalty: Rectification and penalties under the cybersecurity and content-management regime.

obligations.regime: AI Guidelines for Business (soft law) + AI Promotion Act
obligations.citation: METI/MIC, AI Guidelines for Business Ver. 1.2 (main text, 31 Mar 2026) with Appendix Ver. 1.1 (4 Apr 2025); orig. Ver. 1.0, 19 Apr 2024; Act on the Promotion of Research and Development and the Utilization of AI-Related Technologies (AI Promotion Act), in force 4 Jun 2025
obligations.effective_date_note: AI Guidelines for Business introduced 19 Apr 2024 (Ver1.0; current main text Ver1.2, 31 Mar 2026); soft law, no statutory penalties.
requirements: Apply the 10 common guiding principles: (1) human-centric, (2) safety, (3) fairness, (4) privacy protection, (5) ensuring security, (6) transparency, (7) accountability, (8) education/literacy, (9) ensuring fair competition, (10) innovation.
Identify your role -- AI developer, AI provider, or AI business user -- and follow the role-specific actions; obligations flow contractually between these actors.
Conduct agile, risk-based self-governance proportionate to the system's impact, referencing the prior MIC/METI guidelines the document integrates.

Japan's approach is promotion-oriented: the AI Promotion Act sets national policy and a duty to cooperate but creates no detailed prohibitions; risk is managed through existing laws plus ministry guidelines.
obligations.penalty: No direct fines or penalties. The AI Promotion Act relies on government guidance, information requests, and public disclosure of non-compliant businesses ('name and shame'); the AI Strategy Headquarters (chaired by the Prime Minister) coordinates national AI policy.

temporal_model_note: Each obligation carries an `effective_date` (the date it became applicable/enforceable, from primary sources) so audits can be run point-in-time: an audit dated D considers only obligations in force on D, and lists those not yet in force as `pending`. `effective_date_note` records phasing/amendment caveats. Dates are interpretation of published instruments, hand-maintained.
